title: New Primary School Construction Completed in Gonder Ber Kebele
summary: A new 8-classroom primary school has been completed and handed over to the community, serving over 400 students from the surrounding areas.
content: A new 8-classroom primary school has been completed and handed over to the community, serving over 400 students from the surrounding areas. The school includes a library and administrative offices. This project was funded jointly by the regional government and community contributions, demonstrating the power of collaborative development.
author: Communication Office

title: Health Center Expansion Project Launched in Mehal Sayint Town
summary: Construction has begun on expanding the main health center to include maternity and pediatric wards.
content: Construction has begun on expanding the main health center to include maternity and pediatric wards. The project is expected to be completed within 8 months. The expanded facility will serve an additional 15,000 residents and create 25 new healthcare jobs.
author: Health Office

title: Annual Agricultural Input Distribution Campaign Reaches 10,000 Households
summary: Over 10,000 farming households across all 22 kebeles have received improved seeds and fertilizer.
content: Over 10,000 farming households across all 22 kebeles have received improved seeds and fertilizer for the main planting season, boosting food security prospects. The distribution was carried out through the Agricultural Transformation Agency's national program.
author: Agriculture Office

title: Road Construction Connecting 3 Remote Kebeles Approved
summary: The regional government has approved funding for 25 km of new all-weather roads.
content: The regional government has approved funding for 25 km of new all-weather roads connecting remote kebeles to the woreda capital, improving access to services. This project will benefit approximately 30,000 residents.
author: Road & Transport Office

title: Woreda Council Approves Record Annual Budget of ETB 85 Million
summary: The council approved a budget of ETB 85 million for the fiscal year 2026/27.
content: The council approved a budget of ETB 85 million for the fiscal year 2026/27, with major allocations for education, health, and road infrastructure. This represents a 15% increase from the previous fiscal year.
author: Finance Office

title: Clean Water Project Benefits 5,000 Residents in 5 Kebeles
summary: Five new deep boreholes with solar-powered pumps now provide clean, safe water access.
content: Five new deep boreholes with solar-powered pumps now provide clean, safe water access to approximately 5,000 residents in previously underserved communities. The project was implemented in partnership with UNICEF and the regional Water Bureau.
author: Water Office
